summary: Joel Spolsky shares a minor correction to a Māori translation used for the fictional 'Institute for Kiwi Studies,' crediting Professor Ray Harlow for the grammatical fix. The post is essentially a brief, lighthearted aside that includes a few fun facts about kiwi birds and a link to a YouTube video.
key_points: A grammatical error in the Māori translation of the fictional Institute for Kiwi Studies was corrected with help from a linguistics professor
The correct translation is 'Te Tari Mātauranga o te Tokoeka,' referring to the South Island kiwi (Apteryx australis)
The post includes trivia about kiwi bird feeding habits as a playful digression
money_quotes: It turns out the Māori translation we were using for the translation of the fictional Institute for Kiwi Studies had a slight grammatical error.
key_insight: This is a trivial, whimsical aside about a Māori grammar correction for a fictional institute name, with no substantive software or business content.
